evidence.collect-event-and-neighboring-e.title: Collect an Event ID and neighboring product events

steps.instruction: Record the Event Log source, Event ID, level, timestamp, and complete General and Details data.

steps.failure: Return to the complete Event Log detail and configuration export before changing settings.

Remove passwords, private keys, license data, and other secrets from evidence.

product_paths.eventreporter: Configuration Client > Services > Event Log Monitor or Event Log Monitor V2 > affected service.
product_paths.winsyslog: Configuration Client > Services > Event Log Monitor or Event Log Monitor V2 > affected service.
product_paths.mwagent: Configuration Client > Services > Event Log Monitor or Event Log Monitor V2 > affected service.
product_paths.rsyslog-client: Configuration Client > Services > Event Log Monitor or Event Log Monitor V2 > affected service.

steps.instruction: Record exact channel name, collection mode, service account, and intended start position.
steps.expected: The affected object and its effective settings are identified.
steps.failure: Return to the complete Event Log detail and configuration export before changing settings.

steps.instruction: Run the native Windows checks below from the affected product host.
steps.expected: The channel is enabled and readable in the service-account context; a test event is collected once.
steps.failure: Correct the channel name or least-privilege access; reset position only after recording replay impact.

steps.instruction: Perform one uniquely identifiable product test through the same service, rule, or action.
steps.expected: The intended destination records the test exactly once.
steps.failure: Collect the first new product event and bounded debug output; do not change unrelated settings.

eventlog.verify-channel-access-and-bookm.verification: Repeat the affected operation, confirm its positive output, and verify that queues, collection positions, or remote delivery continue normally.
evidence_to_collect: The complete Event Log entry and neighboring product events with timestamps.
The command output, relevant configuration export, and bounded debug log from the same interval.
